notes: This skill drives a live, logged-in Facebook session and writes to a real Google Sheet, so full with-skill/baseline output benchmarks would have side effects (sheet writes, FB requests) and are non-deterministic. The meaningful, safe eval here is TRIGGERING accuracy — does the description fire on the right intents and stay quiet on adjacent ones (leads-to-crm, sales-playbook, Instagram/LinkedIn sourcing). Optimize via scripts/run_loop.py (description improver). The prompts below double as triggering test cases; assertions describe the behavior a correct run should exhibit if executed manually with preflight done.

evals.prompt: The Instant Facebook Leads sheet has a bunch of new group post links in column A with no profile URLs yet. Can you go get the profile URLs for the people who posted them?
evals.expected_output: Invokes facebook-lead-nav: runs preflight, then a dry-run, then a live enrichment capped at ~15, writing Lead Name + Profile URL + Date Added to new columns on each post row; resume-safe.

assertions.text: Does a dry-run before any live write
Caps the batch at roughly 10-20 posts

evals.prompt: run the facebook lead nav for the next 10 posts
push the facebook leads into the outreach CRM and write their first touch message
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke facebook-lead-nav. This is leads-to-crm territory (routing + Touch-1 messaging).
